stimulus: The villanelle, a poetic form originating in sixteenth-century French pastoral verse, has an exacting structure. The contemporary scholar Annie Finch explains that each one of a villanelle’s nineteen lines, while contributing to the patterns of refrain (a recurring first and third line) and rhyme established in the poem’s opening tercet, ______blank rhythmically distinct and tonally deliberate, producing what she calls "a meticulously braided silence."
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is the use of verb forms within a sentence. The singular verb "is" agrees in number with the singular subject "each one of a villanelle’s nineteen lines." While the prepositional phrase "of a villanelle’s nineteen lines" within the subject contains a plural noun, the head of the subject ("each one") is singular, indicating that each individual line (not the lines as a group) is "rhythmically distinct and tonally deliberate."

Choice A is incorrect because the plural verb "have been" doesn’t agree in number with the singular subject "each one of a villanelle’s nineteen lines." Choice C is incorrect because the plural verb "are" doesn’t agree in number with the singular subject. Choice D is incorrect because the plural verb "were" doesn’t agree in number with the singular subject.
